texto: You kissed me.' I tear my eyes away from the mirror to face her. We are close, so close standing here. My lungs might be carved out of granite, cast from bronze, the way they refuse to expand, to fill with air.'You kissed me back,' says Kat softly.I nod. There's nothing more to say.
explicacao: Citação de Elissa Janine Hoole sobre temas ligados a amor. “You kissed me.' I tear my eyes away from the mirror to face her. We are close, so close standing …” articula amor, kissing, love em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como love.

frase_original: You kissed me.' I tear my eyes away from the mirror to face her. We are close, so close standing here. My lungs might be carved out of granite, cast from bronze, the way they refuse to expand, to fill with air.'You kissed me back,' says Kat softly.I nod. There's nothing more to say.

texto: It would not be fair to say that the fire stole my faith, since in truth it has been slipping away from me all my life, flipping between my fingers like a shiny little minnow--such a far cry from the trophy salmon that dangled from my father's fist.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Elissa Janine Hoole: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “It would not be fair to say that the fire stole my faith, since in truth it has been slipping awa…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, metaphor, would pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: It would not be fair to say that the fire stole my faith, since in truth it has been slipping away from me all my life, flipping between my fingers like a shiny little minnow--such a far cry from the trophy salmon that dangled from my father's fist.
